topic: Global mode for cfs where skills and sub-agents are available globally across repositories, while each repository keeps only configuration, lock/update policy, and cache metadata.

decisions.command_targeting: cfs update and cfs generate-agents inspect init mode by default; --local and --global override the target.
decisions.explicit_targeting: cfs update --local|--global and cfs generate-agents --local|--global are supported.
decisions.global_mode: Skills and sub-agents are global; repo stores config, immutable lock/update policy, and cache metadata only.
decisions.host_footprint: Existing {cf-studio-path}/config is the repo discovery/config surface; no extra cfs.toml is required by default.
decisions.init_mode: cfs init creates only {cf-studio-path}/config by default.
decisions.local_init: cfs init --local creates .core and .gen for a fully repo-local installation.
decisions.local_mode: Everything is per-repo, with no dependency on global runtime.
decisions.mixed_mode: Global/config-only and local/per-repo modes are permanent first-class modes.
decisions.offline_policy: Offline startup is allowed only after full hydration and integrity verification of the locked bundle and manifest.
decisions.update_policy: Semver/range policy is used only by cfs update to choose the next lock.

open_questions: Exact core.toml schema for runtime lock and update policy.
Global cache directory layout and bundle identity key.
Where each supported host stores global skills/sub-agents.
cfs info output for active mode, effective runtime source, lock status, and hydration status.
recommended_next_step: Draft a resolution matrix for init, update, generate-agents, runtime lookup, --local, and --global.
